pattern: A frequency prefix-sum over the small value range (0–100) lets you answer 'how many values are strictly smaller' for every element in O(1) after an O(101) prefix pass.
hints: For each element, you want the count of values in the array that are strictly less than it — that is the rank of the element in a sorted view.
Since values only go up to 100, a counting array of size 101 is sufficient; its prefix sum at index v gives the total count of values below v.
Build the frequency table, convert it to a prefix sum, then map each original element through the prefix sum to get its answer.

edge_inputs.description: all elements identical — none is smaller than any other
strictly increasing — each element's answer is its index
minimum array length with two equal elements
single zero — answer for zero is always 0

stress.description: large array with values in the full 0–100 range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [[rng.randint(0, 100) for _ in range(size)]]
